text:
I just posted a comment related to a joke in this video, and YouTube just instantly deleted it, so here it is again, slightly re-worded this time.

The standard NBA basketball has a circumference of 29.5 inches (75 cm), or a diameter of 9.4 inches (23.9 cm).

I hear the human rectum can stretch about 7 inches (17.8 cm) without any permanent damage, but that's a commonly repeated fact due to one post mentioning you can fit a raccoon there. The only other estimate I could find was 6-8 inches (15.2-20.3 cm) from a vice article, which also mentioned doctors have found plenty of strange things in there, including a live eel.

But I didn't quite trust those figures as it’s a huge meme, so I decided to have the search limited to .gov websites, but I couldn't find much of anything relevant there.

Conclusion: You can't fit a basketball up your ass, unless maybe you practice with smaller things and eventually build up to it, which may result in medical complications including, but not limited to, being more prone to shit yourself.

Edit: In other words, "Never put the ball 🏀 up your poop hole" is actually good life advice.

text:
List of callbacks:

0:55 Daddy Dribble's TV head plays the same clip of Howdy Doody as the one from "Too Much Fiddling With The Antenna"

1:49 When the basketball takes out the ceiling, the captions read "[when you need ceiling repair, YOU want ceiling repair]", referencing the opening line to "No one needs foundation repair"

1:53 Two clips from "OUTIE INNIE OUCHIE" is used in the sentence "... you cannot play the game with a basketball."

3:11 A clip from DaThings1's "GET OUT Gasoline Park" is used when Steve is denied entry to the restroom

4:09 The phrase "100% Unsatisfied" is lifted from "No one needs foundation repair"

4:29 This references a running gag started by  YTPer Noisepuppet (later adopted by CS), where a word is reversed in order to form the word 'Toot" while an image of Dr. Doom blowing a horn with the onomatopoeia "Toot!" appears above the horn

4:33 Another clip from "GET OUT Gasoline Park" is used when Steve runs

4:42 The gag of the word "Drills" being repeated thrice in an increasingly high pitch originated in "Billy Mays Career In Television Advertising Hits A Record Low"

4:56 Yet another clip from "GET OUT Gasoline Park" is used when Steve is told to GET OUT of the gymnasium

6:55 A final clip from "GET OUT Gasoline Park" is used when Steve is warned not to hang on the rim while playing basketball at his own risk

7:04 The line "We can put tape on the house" and the respective clip are from "No one needs foundation repair"

8:36 Green Day - "Basketcase" made a short appearance in "Pooping the Charts, Vol. 1990s: PO(O)P ROCK CHAOS!"

Reply if I missed any

EDIT: I missed some.

0:23 When the basketball hits the lens, the captions read "[THAT'LL LEAVE A MARK]", possibly referencing a line from Billy Mays in "Caillou's Ebolaween Twentyfourteen" after Rosie (idiot) runs into a cabinet and leaves a bloodstain.

0:42 The clip of a car driving into a hole and letting out a plume of smoke might be a reference to "OBAMA WIPES UP THE YOUTUBE SH!TDOWN" where the Senate and House of Representatives experience a similar fate (except the hole was filled with fire).

1:03 The clip from "Baby Got Back" of one of the backing singers saying "Gross!" has appeared several times in CS works, though I'm not sure where it started.

1:19 The clip from Billy Mays' pitch for the Big City Slider Station where the graphic reads "Just 2 Minutes" has also been used by CS multiple times.

2:17 I swear I've seen the face on that Boo somewhere. Paula Deen? Gotye? A JOJite? I dunno.

5:38 Another use of the Baby Got Back "Gross!" clip.

6:09 The edit depicting Steve with six arms could be a reference to the similar transformation of Phil Swift in "Flextits Makes You Super Wet and Completely Dry", which also was used as the thumbnail.

EDIT 2: As pointed out by @JetFalcon710, the face on the Boo at 2:17 is Doug DeMuro, who has appeared several times in CS188's vids.

text:
0:41 - I'm glad you called, Daddy Dribble. I've been looking for an extended warranty for my 1999 Toyota Camry LE. Current mileage: 366,782... Hello? Hello?
1:02 - I thought the only vampires that could stand sunlight were the glitterbugs in "Twilight." (By the way, how I un-see that image?)
3:22 - I didn't know Michael Rosen was a fan of ballsketblab. Turns out, he's such a big fan that he takes up half of one bleacher. *tongue click* Noice!
4:16 - Wouldn't fornication in a constipation situation make things WORSE? 🤣



2:15 - About three months ago. Diane was a much-beloved Dunkin' customer, and mother of a child I grew up with - we weren't friends, but we knew each other from grade school and high school bands. My parents attended her wedding. One night, a few weeks before Christmas 2021, she stopped for some coffees and we chatted. She had recently come home from the hospital after a minor back operation, and she was excited to finally experience a pain-free Christmas with her grandchildren for the first time in years. When she left, she parked her Subaru behind our building as she normally did, with her two dogs, and played some games on her mobile phone using our store's WiFi. It was her "me time," and this was a normal thing for her to do several times a week, and she did it year-round. Right before I left for the night, as I tossed the last trash bag in the dumpster, I waved goodnight and she waved back.

I was the last person to see her alive. She passed away quietly in her sleep overnight. Since then, she's appeared for a moment or two if I'm there really late at night by myself. Whenever she appears I feel comforted, it's almost like she's checking up on me. If I stay far too late, she'll knock something over and I'll hear her laugh, and I'll say out loud, "OK, Diane, you win. I'll go home now." And I do, because I hate picking up dishes and re-washing them because she's telling me, in no uncertain terms, to GO HOME to my family.

Had I known that was her last night, I would have told her the same thing.

text:
Ok so game theory.. Steve is the reincarnation of Jesus and the Coach is god himself. Let me explain: 

Steve seems to have ungodly magic that damages buildings by him throwing a basketball. He's considered a "god" and that he's "perfect". But his arrogance gets the best of him and he abuses his powers for his entertainment. 

Remember when the coach said that "you die for your coach"? He's referring to himself, Steve, aka Jesus, died for our sins. Though Steve has godly powers, the coach can control everything around him. That's why he gave Steve power ups and turned the floor into water. In this video, they show that the coach is more of a fatherly figure in Steve's life. He scolds and talks to him as if he's his son. 

They also talk about temptation. The man touching Steve's balls is the representation of the devil. In the beginning of the video, the coach tells us that we could be "a winner or a sinner, it's our choice."  

But hey yey... that's just a theory, a gey theory!
